agency: New York City Department of Investigation (DOI)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/doi (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace). Followed the 'Report Corruption' → 'Submit Report Online' path to its host — a third-party Kaseware intake portal (app.kaseware.us/public/#NYCDOI/..., Cloudflare-fronted, title 'Kaseware Portal'). Confirmed DOI's public reports are PDFs served from nyc.gov/assets/doi/reports/pdf/<year>/. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Investigation (DOI)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 4 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: DOI is New York City's Inspector General, but its core work product — public investigation reports and reform recommendations — is published ONLY as PDFs (nyc.gov/assets/doi/reports/pdf/<year>/), with no structured Open Data twin and no content API.

DOI's four Open Data datasets are not its investigations: they are dominated by City Marshal oversight — executed Evictions (6z8x-wfk4, ~237k views, DOI's most-viewed asset) and City Marshals Revenue — plus the Policy & Procedure Recommendations tracker and monthly performance indicators.

The single most important inbound transaction — reporting fraud, waste, or corruption — runs through a THIRD-PARTY vendor form: Kaseware (app.kaseware.us), a case-management SaaS, with phone/fax/mail as fallbacks. There is no API.
The one genuinely structured investigative-output twin is the PPR Portal (jstn-jaut): DOI's recommendations to agencies plus acceptance and implementation status.
Net-new write surface: file a corruption complaint (CorruptionComplaint) — the tip that starts every investigation, today locked in the Kaseware portal. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 4 DOI datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. Mostly City Marshal oversight (evictions, revenue) plus PPR recommendations and performance indicators — not the investigations themselves.
The 'Submit Report Online' corruption-complaint form. Third-party Kaseware Portal SPA; no JSON/OpenAPI surface. This is the net-new write gap.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Livesite v22, Dynatrace RUM. Public reports served as static PDFs under /assets/doi/reports/pdf/. No content API.
referenced as an alternate reporting channel for some complaint types.

fruit.name: Public Investigation Reports
fruit.notes: DOI's core work product — investigative findings and reform recommendations. Published only as PDFs under /assets/doi/reports/pdf/<year>/ (e.g. 2025 Annual Anti-Corruption Report, the NYCHA vacant-apartment reclamation report, OIG-NYPD annual reports). No structured data, no API — the central finding.

fruit.name: Policy & Procedure Recommendations (PPR)
opendata_match: Policy and Procedure Recommendations (PPR) Portal jstn-jaut (7c)
fruit.notes: The one genuinely structured investigative-output twin: recommendations DOI issues to agencies, with acceptance status, agency-reported status, and implementation status/date.

fruit.name: Evictions (City Marshal oversight)
fruit.notes: DOI's most-viewed dataset by far. Executed residential/commercial evictions by City Marshals (the officials DOI appoints and oversees). Full NYC geography spine (BBL, BIN, census tract, council district, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: City Marshals Revenue
fruit.notes: Annual fee, poundage, assessment fee, and net income per marshal — DOI's financial oversight of the City Marshals it regulates.

fruit.name: Monthly Performance Management Reports
fruit.notes: DOI operational metrics reported to the Mayor's Office of Operations — includes corruption-lecture counts and customer-service indicators.

fruit.name: Report Corruption (online complaint)
fruit.notes: The 'Submit Report Online' path from nyc.gov/site/doi/report/report-corruption. Runs on Kaseware, a third-party case-management SaaS; no API. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain — the tip that starts every investigation.

fruit.name: Whistleblower / EO-16 reporting obligation
fruit.notes: City employees must report allegations of corruption to DOI (Executive Order 16); the City's Whistleblower Law protects against retaliation. Same Kaseware/phone/fax/mail channels — no machine-readable path.

fruit.name: OIG-NYPD complaint
fruit.notes: The Office of the Inspector General for the NYPD routes complaints through the same Report-Corruption channels — a specialized flavor of the net-new CorruptionComplaint write surface.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' shared chassis (Akamai + nginx + Livesite v22 + Dynatrace) for the informational site; public reports are static PDFs under /assets/doi/reports/pdf/. The complaint-intake service layer is Kaseware, a third-party case-management SaaS (app.kaseware.us, Cloudflare-fronted) — a public web form with no API.
meta.netnew: CorruptionComplaint — file and track a fraud/waste/corruption tip (with anonymous filing and Whistleblower-Law protection), the transaction that starts every DOI investigation and today lives only in a vendor Kaseware form.
meta.tagline: The city's Inspector General publishes its investigations only as PDFs and takes its complaints through a third-party vendor form.
meta.headline: DOI's core outputs — public investigation reports and the corruption complaints that start them — have no structured, owned, or agent-native surface: the reports are PDFs and the intake is a Kaseware vendor form, so the work is to digitize both.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis for the info site, but no DOI-owned application; the complaint service layer is a third-party vendor SaaS (Kaseware).
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content API; the site is HTML and the reports are static PDFs.
scorecard_notes.openData: 4 datasets — genuinely useful (Evictions is a ~237k-view asset) but mostly City Marshal oversight, not DOI's investigations.
scorecard_notes.coreData: The core object — a public investigation report — is published only as PDF, never structured; PPR gives a partial structured recommendation twin.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: None — filing a corruption complaint is a Kaseware vendor form plus phone/fax/mail.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no clean OpenAPI, no structured report or complaint contract.
scorecard_notes.ownership: DOI owns the marshal/eviction and PPR data (open), but its investigative reports are PDFs and its complaint intake is leased to a third-party vendor.
